mnemonics: U.E. = U + E: Usability (can they use it) plus Engagement (do they enjoy it) equals User Experience
keyPoints: Usability is a component of user experience (UX), focused on effectiveness, efficiency, and satisfaction in achieving goals.
User experience (UX) is broader, encompassing all aspects of the user's interaction with the company, its services, and its products.
A usable product is not necessarily a good user experience; UX also includes emotion, enjoyment, and aesthetic appeal.
commonMistakes: Equating usability with the entire user experience, ignoring emotional and hedonic qualities.
Prioritizing aesthetic design over core usability, making a product beautiful but difficult to use.
Assuming that if a product is usable, the user experience is automatically positive.
